'''KT-10''' was an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] with a [[Programmer/Legends|programmed]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] personality. She was captured by the giant [[Droid/Legends|droid]] known as the [[Great Heep]] on [[Biitu]], and she and fellow astromech [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] became romantically interested in each other while the two were being held captive in the Heep's [[droid harem]]. KT-10 was eventually fed to the Heep, had her power drained, and was deactivated. R2-D2 and the Heep's other astromech droid [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] rescued her from the junkyard where she had been deposited and gave their power up to reactivate her.

==Biography==
[[File:KTAndR2-TGH.png|thumb|left|220px|KT-10 and R2-D2 begin their relationship]]
KT-10,<ref name="GL">''[[George Lucas: The Creative Impulse]]''</ref> an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] with [[Gender/Legends|feminine]] [[Programmer/Legends|programming]], found herself on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Biitu]]<ref name="The Great Heep" /> in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]]<ref name="P170">{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}}</ref> when she was captured by the [[Great Heep]], a giant [[Droid/Legends|droid]] dispatched by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to drain the [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworld]] of its resources. KT-10 was placed in the [[droid harem]], a location in the Heep's base where he kept the astromech droids upon whose power he would regularly feed. There, like the other droids in the harem, she was pampered with [[Oil bath/Legends|oil baths]] and cushions to keep her unaware of her eventual fate. KT-10 attracted the attention of many of the other masculine-programmed droids there. While watching her, they found themselves distracted and prone to accidents.<ref name="The Great Heep" />

When the astromech droid [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] was captured by the Great Heep, he was also deposited into the Heep's harem. KT-10, ignoring the attentions of the other droids, playfully cavorted at the edge of the oil bath that R2 was in. Attracted to her, the other droid approached her and connected his [[Computer/Legends|computer]] [[Scomp link/Legends|interface arm]] to hers. Despite this earning the jealousy of other droids in the harem, the two moved into the oil bath to play and spray oil at each other.<ref name="The Great Heep" />

[[File:KTRevived-TGH.png|thumb|right|220px|KT-10 is helped up after being reactivated]]
Over the course of the next [[Standard day/Legends|day]], the two became very friendly, playing and nuzzling each other in the pools of oil. This happiness was not to last, however. The Great Heep needed more power, and wanted another R2 unit. R2-D2's [[Counterpart/Legends|counterpart]], the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], had meanwhile found his way into the harem to rescue R2-D2. When R2-D2 left the oil bath to speak to him, KT-10 was captured. She was taken to the Heep and fed to him, with the two other droids only able to hear her scream. KT-10's shell was deposited in a junkyard.<ref name="The Great Heep" />

In his attempt to escape, R2-D2 found his way to the same junkyard, where he found the remains of KT-10. He attempted to use his own power to reactivate her, but did not have enough power, and was also soon taken captive by the Great Heep. When the Great Heep was finally defeated and his droids set free, they and R2-D2 crowded around the empty shell of KT-10. R2-D2 again connected his power supply to her, only this time all the other droids linked up to him, so he could use their power as well. This was enough, and soon KT-10 was reactivated. She and R2-D2 reunited at last.<ref name="The Great Heep" /> Their relationship would not last, though. R2-D2 enjoyed adventure too much to settle down, and the two droids parted ways.<ref name="Databank" />

==Characteristics==
Considered to be an offshoot of the [[KT-series]] model of astromech droid,<ref name="Blog">{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-2|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref> KT-10 was an ovoid astromech droid decorated in [[Color/Legends|pink]] and red. Her sensors were blue, and the treads on the bottom of her [[Limb/Legends|legs]] resembled women's footwear. KT-10, despite being considered attractive by other astromech droids, was somewhat coy in her flirtations with R2-D2. Nonetheless, she allowed their relationship to advance very quickly, to the extent that R2-D2 was willing to sacrifice himself to save her.<ref name="The Great Heep" />

==Behind the scenes==
KT-10 first appeared in ''[[The Great Heep]]'', a [[1986]] TV special based on the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] animated series. In ''The Great Heep'', KT-10 is brought to the Heep when he demands "another [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2 unit]];" throughout the short film, astromech droids of all shapes and sizes are collectively called "R2 units."<ref name="The Great Heep" /> The [[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded|second]]<ref name="Guide 2">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> and [[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded|third]] editions of ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'',<ref name="Guide 3">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' all label her as an R2 unit,<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|233|KT-10}}</ref> even though her appearance and designation do not match the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2 series]].<ref name="The Great Heep" /> Unnamed in the episode, the droid was given a name in [[1992]]'s ''[[George Lucas: The Creative Impulse]]''.<ref name="GL" />
